## 4. Assets

Information assets and the classification level that drives the Confidentiality / Integrity / Availability targets used in [§8 Findings Register](#8-findings-register) risk scoring.

| Asset | ID | Classification | Description | Linked Threats |
|--------------------|------|------------|----------------------------------------|----------------------|
| User Credentials (email + password hash) | A-001 | Restricted | User account email addresses and MD5-hashed passwords stored in SQLite Users table. MD5 hashing makes these trivially reversible. Used for authentication. | [F-001](#f-001) · [F-005](#f-005) · [F-007](#f-007) · [F-009](#f-009) · [F-018](#f-018) · [F-030](#f-030) · [F-037](#f-037) |
| JWT RSA Private Key | A-002 | Restricted | RSA private key used to sign all JWT tokens, hardcoded in lib/insecurity.ts:26. Any party with access to source code can sign arbitrary JWTs as any user including admin. | [F-002](#f-002) · [F-003](#f-003) · [F-004](#f-004) · [F-007](#f-007) · [F-014](#f-014) · [F-021](#f-021) · [F-036](#f-036) · [F-041](#f-041) |
| User Personal Data (PII) | A-003 | Restricted | User profile data including full name, address, credit card numbers, mobile numbers stored across multiple Sequelize models (Address, Card, User). | [F-001](#f-001) · [F-005](#f-005) · [F-013](#f-013) · [F-018](#f-018) · [F-019](#f-019) · [F-024](#f-024) · [F-033](#f-033) · [F-037](#f-037) |
| Payment Card Data | A-004 | Restricted | Credit card numbers (CardNum), expiry months/years stored in Card model without encryption. Includes test cards like 4716190207394368. | [F-001](#f-001) · [F-005](#f-005) · [F-013](#f-013) · [F-018](#f-018) · [F-019](#f-019) · [F-024](#f-024) · [F-033](#f-033) · [F-037](#f-037) |
| TOTP Secrets (2FA Seeds) | A-010 | Restricted | TOTP secret seeds stored in Users.totpSecret field in SQLite. These are the 2FA enrollment keys that, if extracted, allow bypassing 2FA. | — |
| User Security Answers | A-012 | Restricted | Answers to security questions stored in SecurityAnswer model, used for password reset flow. Predictable answers enable account takeover without knowing password. | [F-001](#f-001) · [F-005](#f-005) · [F-007](#f-007) · [F-009](#f-009) · [F-018](#f-018) · [F-030](#f-030) · [F-037](#f-037) |
| Confidential Business Documents | A-005 | Confidential | M&A acquisition plans in /ftp/acquisitions.md, incident support KeePass database at /ftp/incident-support.kdbx, and coupon codes in /ftp/coupons_2013.md.bak. All publicly accessible via directory listing. | [F-006](#f-006) · [F-013](#f-013) · [F-019](#f-019) · [F-020](#f-020) · [F-021](#f-021) · [F-033](#f-033) · [F-036](#f-036) · [F-041](#f-041) |
| Application Source Code and Config | A-006 | Confidential | Node.js TypeScript source including security library (lib/insecurity.ts with hardcoded private key, HMAC secret). Config files exposing Google OAuth client ID and server configuration. | [F-002](#f-002) · [F-003](#f-003) · [F-007](#f-007) · [F-014](#f-014) · [F-034](#f-034) · [F-036](#f-036) · [F-041](#f-041) |
| SQLite Database File | A-007 | Confidential | data/juiceshop.sqlite contains all user data, orders, and challenge state. The challenge tracking table reveals which challenges each user has solved. | [F-024](#f-024) |
| Access Logs | A-008 | Internal | HTTP access logs stored in logs/access.log.* and accessible via /support/logs directory listing. Reveals user activity patterns, IP addresses, and browsed URLs. | [F-006](#f-006) · [F-013](#f-013) · [F-019](#f-019) · [F-020](#f-020) · [F-021](#f-021) · [F-033](#f-033) · [F-041](#f-041) |
| Product Catalog and Pricing | A-009 | Internal | Product names, descriptions, prices, and quantities stored in SQLite Products table. Modifiable by authenticated users due to intentionally commented-out authorization. | [F-001](#f-001) · [F-005](#f-005) · [F-013](#f-013) · [F-018](#f-018) · [F-037](#f-037) |
| Application Availability | A-011 | Internal | The running Juice Shop instance serving training content. DoS via RCE, XML billion-laughs, ZIP bomb, or NoSQL sleep injection can make it unavailable. | — |
